title: Email Forwarding Guide link is a placeholder
detail: The per-provider forwarding guide (Gmail / Outlook web / classic Outlook) is referenced but the URL is a placeholder (“FORWARDING GUIDE LINK”).
suggestion: Publish the real guide and store the link in the Reference Sheet and the Resources page.

title: Historical file upload procedure
detail: No process, supported formats, size limits, or “when is this in scope” guidance for historical files.
suggestion: Define scope criteria, supported formats, size limits, and a step-by-step upload flow; add it to File Loading.

title: Current project file loading
detail: No defined internal procedure for how files get into a project (the source only describes the client “running a project”).
suggestion: Document the internal project-file loading steps and expected placement, then link from File Loading.

title: Indexing / processing expectations
detail: No expected processing time or a clear way to tell when files are fully ready vs stuck.
suggestion: Publish typical processing windows by file size and a definitive 'ready' signal to check.

title: Organization creation mechanics
detail: No admin steps for creating parent/child orgs, and no explicit definition of the parent↔child model beyond “offices, line cards, user hierarchies.”
suggestion: Write the exact admin steps to create parent/child orgs and define the hierarchy model precisely.

title: Credential generation trigger
detail: The client “receives credentials by email,” but the internal step that triggers that email isn't documented.
suggestion: Document the exact action that sends credential emails and how to re-send on bounce.

title: Role / permission catalog
detail: The specific roles and permission levels available — and what each grants — aren't listed.
suggestion: Publish a role matrix (role → permissions granted) so assignment is deterministic.

title: Pilot vs Full License differences
detail: What actually changes in setup between the two account types isn't specified.
suggestion: List the concrete feature/seat differences so the account type drives clear setup choices.

title: Supported CRMs
detail: Which CRM systems are supported and the auth method for each is not documented.
suggestion: Maintain a supported-CRM list with the auth method and required permissions per system.

title: Internal RACI
detail: The source lists client-facing roles but not who internally owns each build task. Ownership in this playbook is a recommended default.
suggestion: Ratify the per-task owners (CS/OPS/ENG/EXEC) with the team and lock them in.

title: Who sends the welcome package
detail: The owner and trigger point for sending the welcome package aren't specified.
suggestion: Assign an owner and a definite trigger (e.g. immediately after CS sign-off).

title: Data security / access provisioning
detail: No documented least-privilege standard or offboarding process.
suggestion: Define least-privilege defaults per role and a deprovisioning checklist for departing users.

title: Error handling / rollback
detail: No guidance for what to do when ingestion, CRM sync, or invites fail.
suggestion: Add failure playbooks (the Troubleshooting page is a start) and rollback steps per integration.

title: Definition of “done”
detail: No formal go-live acceptance criteria or client sign-off artifact.
suggestion: Create a go-live acceptance checklist and a recorded sign-off artifact per client.

title: Time estimates
detail: The source gives no per-task durations. Estimates in this playbook are planning defaults.
suggestion: Calibrate the estimates against real setups and adjust per account size.

title: Escalation thresholds
detail: When a stuck setup task should be escalated to the Support Engineer or COO isn't defined.
suggestion: Set explicit time/severity thresholds that trigger escalation to ENG or EXEC.
